english: measure word for trees and plants

etymology.description: semantic 木(木) (mù, "wood; tree") + phonetic 可(可) (kě) -> meaning measure word for trees and plants
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 木(木) (mù, "wood; tree"), indicating its relation to trees and plants, with 可(可) (kě) for its phonetic component. The semantic 木 reinforces the association with objects made of or related to wood, like trees and plants.
etymology.simplification: Simplified as 棵 by reducing the complexity of certain strokes in 樖, keeping the tree radical for semantic clarity while using 可 for phonetic consistency.

examples.chinese: 院子里有一棵大树(院子裡有一棵大樹)。
examples.pinyin: Yuànzi lǐ yǒu yī kē dà shù.
examples.english: There is a big tree in the yard.

examples.chinese: 我种了一棵苹果树(我種了一棵蘋果樹)，它已经开花了(它已經開花了)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zhòngle yī kē píngguǒshù, tā yǐjīng kāihuā le.
examples.english: I planted an apple tree, and it has already blossomed.

examples.chinese: 花园里有几棵玫瑰花(花園裡有幾棵玫瑰花)，它们开得很漂亮(它們開得很漂亮)。
examples.pinyin: Huāyuán lǐ yǒu jǐ kē méiguīhuā, tāmen kāi de hěn piàoliang.
examples.english: There are several rose bushes in the garden, and they bloom beautifully.
